  University assignee: 0.0342   (PAPER_REPORTED: positive)

=== CONCLUSION ===
The synthetic data analysis supports the paper's main finding:
  * Government interest (federal funding) is positively associated with
    the CD5 index, indicating that federally funded inventions tend to be
    more destabilizing to existing technology streams.
  * Patents assigned to firms tend to have lower (more negative) CD5 scores,
    meaning they are more consolidating of the status quo.
  * University-assigned patents show a positive association with CD5
    (destabilizing).
  * In contrast, all three assignee types (especially firms and universities)
    are positively associated with raw impact (I5), illustrating that
    forward-citation-based measures alone cannot distinguish between
    consolidating and destabilizing inventions.
  * The CDt index thus captures conceptually important differences that are
    invisible to established patent impact measures.
